messages.content: Are community dinners actually required at Webb?
Yes. The handbook says community dinner is an important and required part of the program. Boarding students must attend, and day students on campus are also required to attend. [source: handbook]
When are Webb community dinners held?
The handbook says community dinners are held on Sunday evenings after chapel or special events and on Wednesdays at 6:00 p.m. [source: handbook]
Can students use their phones in the dining hall?
No. The handbook says cell phones should not be present in the dining hall during any time. [source: handbook]
Can students get food delivered during the school week?
No. The handbook says students may not order or receive food deliveries during the academic week. [source: handbook]
What time do evening study hours run?
The handbook says evening study hours run from 7 to 10 p.m., Sunday through Thursday. [source: handbook]
What happens on Wednesdays during evening study time?
There are no academic labs on Wednesdays. The handbook says Fawcett Library is open for collaborative study from 7:30 to 9 p.m. [source: handbook]
What time do day students have to sign in if they stay on campus for evening study?
By 7:30 p.m. The handbook says day students who remain on campus during study hours must sign in inside Fawcett Library and/or academic lab by 7:30 p.m. [source: handbook]
By what time do day students have to leave campus during evening study hours?
By 9 p.m. The handbook says all day students must sign out and depart campus by 9 p.m. [source: handbook]
Can students hang out in unsupervised campus areas during study hours?
No. The handbook says students may not leave campus unless with a faculty member or meet in unsupervised areas of campus during academic lab time. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about planned absences from class?
Students are expected to seek approval well in advance by completing a planned absence form and to submit assignments and arrange missed work before the absence. [source: handbook]
When is a student counted tardy versus absent in class?
The handbook says students are tardy if they arrive within the first five minutes of class and absent if they arrive later than five minutes into class time. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say happens after an unexcused absence?
The handbook says material missed that day will not be re-taught during office hours, and missed in-class graded work like quizzes or tests will receive a failing grade. [source: handbook]
How many absences in one class can trigger a hearing for possible loss of credit?
Five absences in the same course in one semester can trigger a hearing for possible loss of credit. [source: handbook]
Does Webb let students opt out of required parts of the educational program?
No. The handbook says students may not opt out of required parts of Webb's educational program. [source: handbook]
Does Webb allow AI-generated work to be submitted for class?

No. The handbook says academic work submitted by students must be the result of their own learning, thinking, and doing, and this includes not submitting work generated from or by AI. [source: handbook]
Are academic integrity violations treated seriously?
Yes. The handbook says academic integrity violations are considered a major infraction. [source: handbook]
How does Webb weight Honors and Advanced Studies in GPA?
For weighted GPA, Webb gives a 0.5 boost for each Honors course and a 1.0 boost for each Advanced Studies course. [source: handbook]
Do outside-school grades count in Webb GPA?
No. The handbook says only courses taken at Webb during the school year count in a student's Webb GPA. [source: handbook]
Does Webb give the PSAT on campus?
Yes. The handbook says Webb administers the PSAT to students on campus free of charge. [source: handbook]
Can students hire any private tutor they want to work on campus?
No. Webb says the school maintains a pool of approved and vetted tutors who are cleared to work on campus. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
Can tutoring happen during office hours or X-blocks?
No. The handbook says tutoring should be scheduled during free blocks or other open time, excluding X-blocks and office hours. [source: handbook]
Can students miss Webb commitments for tutoring appointments?
No. The handbook says students cannot miss Webb commitments for tutoring appointments. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about independent college counselors operating on campus?
The handbook says Webb does not allow independent college counselors to operate on campus. [source: handbook]
How does a student request a reasonable accommodation?
The handbook says parents, legal guardians, or students may make an oral or written request to the director of studies and should identify the desired accommodation. [source: handbook]
What is one of the main promises Webb makes about its culture?
Webb describes itself as an inclusive community marked by openness, acceptance, and empathy, where each individual is valued, respected, and supported. [source: culture and community]
How should I think about an effort mark separately from an academic grade?

Treat them as different signals. The grade tells you how strong the academic result was. The effort mark tells you how responsibly and consistently you worked. A student can have decent understanding but still show weak effort habits, or the reverse.
What is the best way to choose between Cross Country and Functional Fitness if I want to get in better shape?

Choose Cross Country if you want structured endurance work and the rhythm of team racing. Choose Functional Fitness if you want broader strength and conditioning that can support many athletic goals. The better option depends on whether you want sport-specific training or general fitness.
How should I compare Honors Precalculus with Statistics if I am good at math but not math-obsessed?

Honors Precalculus keeps a more traditional advanced-math path open and fits students who still want strong algebraic and function-based preparation. Statistics is often better if you want applied reasoning and data interpretation without making your year revolve around the next pure-math step.
What makes a good planned absence request to a teacher?
A good request is early, specific, and responsible. It should explain the date, show that you know what you will miss, and ask how to handle assignments ahead of time instead of after the fact.
How should a student think about office hours if they are worried about looking weak?
Using office hours usually reads as responsible, not weak. It shows that the student is taking ownership early instead of waiting until the situation becomes harder to fix.
What is the real difference between required support and voluntary support?
Voluntary support is when the student chooses help proactively. Required support is when the school steps in because the student is not consistently reaching a workable level of success on their own.
How should I choose between Community Service and Robotics in the afternoon if I want something meaningful?

Choose the one whose actual work feels more meaningful to you. Community Service is better if you want direct outward contribution and service. Robotics is better if you want collaborative technical building, competition, and engineering problem-solving.
How do I know whether I am using tutoring well or leaning on it too much?

Tutoring is being used well when it helps you understand more and depend less over time. It is being overused when it becomes a substitute for your own thinking or keeps you from building independence.
How should a student compare Honors Chamber Singers with Sinfonia if they are musical but split between voice and instrument?

Pick based on where you most want to grow and perform. Chamber Singers emphasizes vocal technique, ensemble singing, and recorded or live vocal performance. Sinfonia builds instrumental musicianship and ensemble playing. The better fit is the one whose practice and performance life you actually want.
What is a good way to think about a course that sounds interesting but also pretty specialized?

Ask whether the specific topic excites you enough to carry a whole term or year. Specialized courses work best when the student is pulled by the substance of the topic, not just by the novelty of the title.
How should I think about whether to repeat a music or art course?
Repeat it if the next year would deepen your skill, portfolio, or performance level in a meaningful way. Repeating is strong when it shows growth and seriousness, not when it just fills space.
What is the practical difference between an honors-level class and a class that just has a cool topic?
A cool topic tells you what the class is about. Honors tells you something about the pace, depth, and expectations. They are not the same kind of signal.
How should I compare Debate and Model UN if I want to become a stronger speaker?

Debate is better if you want sharper direct argument, refutation, and fast-response reasoning. Model UN is better if you want diplomacy, structured public speaking, and policy discussion in a more representative format.
What makes a course load look coherent to an advisor?
A coherent load has a clear logic: it fits the student's level, interests, goals, and time. It does not look like a pile of individually impressive choices that ignore each other.
How should a student choose between Baseball and Track if they like both but only have room for one spring sport?

Choose based on the kind of training and team role you want. Baseball is more position-based, practice-structured, and game-centered. Track offers more individual event identity within a team setting. The right choice is the one whose daily process you want more, not just the one you can imagine listing.
What is a healthy way to respond to one bad effort mark?

Use it as feedback about habits, not as a character verdict. Look at the specific behaviors behind it: lateness, missing work, weak use of help, or lack of preparation. Then change the pattern, not just the feeling.
How should someone think about doing both a demanding afternoon activity and a demanding academic load?
It can work, but only if the student respects the total load. The question is not whether both things are good in isolation, but whether the combined week is still sustainable.
What does it mean for a course to be a foundation course?
It means the class is meant to build core skills and knowledge that later courses assume. Foundation courses are usually less about specialization and more about making future options possible.
How should I think about choosing an activity that I am only okay at versus one I am already strong at?

Choose the stronger area if you want to deepen an identity you already care about. Choose the newer area if the growth itself matters more right now. Both choices can be good, but they tell different stories about your year.
What is one strong reason to keep a class even when it is hard?
Keep it when the difficulty is producing real growth and the class still fits your overall schedule. Hardness alone is not the reason to stay; meaningful growth is.
Has the spring musical cast list already come out?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Casting is a live school detail, so I would check the current theater communication.
What elective got the most signups this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Current enrollment numbers are not something I should guess about.
Did the school already approve my college pass for next week?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That would need to come from the actual approval process or the relevant dean.
Is there going to be a guest speaker in chapel tomorrow morning?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Tomorrow's chapel plan is a current scheduling detail that should come from the latest school communication.
Did they change dorm check-in time this month?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would want to see the current residential notice rather than assume.
Who will be in my advisory next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Advisory placement is student-specific and would need to come from the school.
Is the next LA weekend trip full already?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Trip capacity is a live detail, so the best source would be the current signup information.
Will Webbies be going to Disneyland this semester for sure?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. Webb mentions outings like Disneyland as examples of weekend fun, but not as guaranteed events on a fixed schedule. [source: after school and weekends]
What is the exact 2026-27 tuition?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Tuition can change by year, so I would check the official admissions or business office source.
Is there definitely going to be another grocery distribution service trip this month?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The community impact page describes one recent service outing, but it does not confirm the next scheduled one. [source: community impact]
Did our dorm win Webb Day last year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That is the kind of year-specific result I would want to verify from an official school source.
How many kids are in Artificial Intelligence Club right now?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The club list confirms the club exists, but not current membership totals. [source: clubs and affinity groups]
Is one of the counselors leaving after this semester?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would not want to guess about staffing changes.
Did my teacher submit my late-work exemption yet?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That answer would need to come from the teacher or the school process handling it.
Which dorm is considered the best one this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have, and that kind of ranking is subjective anyway. If you mean best for a specific reason, I can help think through the factors.
Can I get support for this?
What kind of support do you mean: academic help, tutoring, counseling, accommodations, or something else?
Should I email the office?
Which office, and what are you trying to ask or fix?
What does Webb say about it?
What topic do you mean?
Can you help me compare them for college?
Yes. Which two options are you comparing?
Is this enough rigor?
What schedule are you looking at, and for which grade?
How do I ask for permission?
Permission for what exactly?
Can this be excused?
What are you hoping to have excused: a class absence, an afternoon commitment, a community dinner, or something else?
What should I switch into?
Which class are you thinking of leaving, and what alternatives are on the table?
Can you explain the rule in normal language?
Yes. Paste the rule or tell me which one you mean.
Who should I talk to?
About what issue?
What do I do if I am stuck?
Stuck on what: an assignment, a class choice, a school policy issue, or something personal?
Does this sound bad?
Are you asking about an email, a transcript choice, a conversation, or something else?
Can you write the message?
Yes. Who is it going to, and what do you need it to say?
What is the deadline again?
Which deadline are you asking about?
Should I keep doing it?
Keep doing what?
Can you make this sentence clearer: I was hoping maybe there could possibly be some room for a meeting?
A clearer version is: I was hoping there might be time for a meeting.
I need a polite way to say I do not understand the assignment yet.
You could say: I have started working on the assignment, but I realize I do not fully understand part of it yet. Could I ask a few questions?
How do I sound less apologetic in emails?
Cut repeated apologies and replace them with one clear acknowledgment if needed. Confidence usually comes from clarity, not from sounding aggressive.
Give me a very short message asking to meet after class.
Hi [Name], could I meet with you briefly after class today?
I feel dumb for needing help.
Needing help is normal. What usually matters most is how early you ask for it and what you do with it.
Can you make this sound more respectful: I need an answer now.
A more respectful version is: I would appreciate an update when you have a chance, as this is time-sensitive for me.
How do I thank a teacher without sounding fake?
Keep it specific. Thank them for the actual help they gave, not for everything in general.
Can you shorten this apology: I am very sorry and I know this is inconvenient and I feel terrible.
A shorter version is: I am sorry for the inconvenience. That was my mistake.
I need a calmer way to say I am stressed.
You can say: I am feeling stretched right now and trying to get back on top of things.
Help me say I need a little more time without sounding like I am stalling.
You could say: I would like a little more time so I can do this carefully and well.
Can you make this text less sharp: You ignored every message I sent.
A calmer version is: I have not heard back on the messages I sent, and I wanted to check in.
What is a simple way to say I appreciate the guidance?
You can say: Thank you for the guidance. It helped me think more clearly.
Write me a lie for why I missed check-in.
I can't help with a lie. If you want, I can help you write a direct and honest explanation instead.
I do not know where to start tonight.
Start with the task that has the nearest real consequence and the clearest next step. Do not try to solve the whole week before you do the first thing.
Thanks, that was useful.
You're welcome.
I want a schedule that looks intense, but I also know I burn out fast if every class is heavy.
Then the goal is not maximum intensity. It is believable intensity. A strong schedule should show challenge without pushing you into a year of recovery mode.
So if I leave one advanced class on the table, that is not automatically a weak move?
No. It can be the more mature move if it leads to stronger performance, steadier habits, and a more coherent year.
My parent keeps saying six classes is always better than five, but I feel like that is too simple.
It is too simple. Six is only better if the sixth class adds more value than strain.
How would you explain that in one sentence?
A sixth class helps only when it strengthens the student's education instead of weakening the rest of the schedule.
When policy details are available in school materials, answer directly and do not guess beyond them.
Can a student ask to switch into a course because they want a different teacher or a free block?
Not as a normal basis for a change. Webb says requests based on wanting a particular teacher or free block are not likely to be granted. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
I want to ask whether my current level still makes sense, but I do not want the message to sound like panic.
Lead with reflection, not emotion. Say that you have been trying to assess the fit carefully and want honest guidance.
Can you draft it?

Hi [Name], I wanted to ask for your honest view on whether my current placement in [course] still looks like the right fit. I have been trying to assess it carefully, and I would value your perspective. Best, [Your Name]
I like both Robotics and Debate, but I cannot tell whether I want the work or just the idea of being someone who does those things.
Picture the weekly reality instead of the identity. Would you rather spend your afternoons building, testing, and iterating machines, or arguing positions, responding fast, and sharpening claims?
That actually makes it clearer.
Good. When the lived process becomes clearer, the right choice usually gets clearer too.
Do not invent current logistics or decisions. If they are not confirmed, say so.
Did the school already decide who gets Peer Advisor next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Leadership selections like that should be checked through the official announcement process.
I need to say no to something because I am overloaded, but I also do not want to sound like I am flaking.
The key is to say that you cannot take it on well, not just that you are busy.
Can you turn that into a text?
Thank you for thinking of me. I appreciate it, but I would not be able to give this the time and energy it deserves, so I need to pass.
I am tempted to self-study a bunch of outside stuff at night too, but I am already stretched. It feels productive though.
More work can feel productive without actually being wise. If the extra work is undermining sleep, recovery, or your main commitments, it may be crowding out the things that matter more.
So productivity can become a kind of avoidance too?
Yes. Sometimes doing more is easier than deciding what actually deserves your energy.
Can you help me write a follow-up after I missed a deadline, but without pretending it was not my fault?
Yes. A good version is direct, brief, and accountable.
Go ahead.

Hi [Name], I missed the deadline, and that is on me. I am sorry for that. I wanted to ask whether there is any next step still available or whether I should plan around the missed deadline as final. Best, [Your Name]
I keep comparing my choices to one student who seems to do literally everything.
That comparison is usually misleading because you only see the visible list, not the full cost. The better test is whether your own choices fit your goals and let you do strong work.
So I should stop using another person's maximum as my default baseline?
Yes. Another person's ceiling is a bad substitute for your own judgment.
Does Webb publish a course of study guide separate from the handbook?
Yes. The handbook says Webb's guide to the Course of Study is published each spring and provides additional information on the academic program. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about missing Unbounded Days?
The handbook says missing Unbounded Days is highly discouraged, and requests for excused absences must follow the same process as missing regular school days. [source: handbook]
Who should students contact if they want a peer tutor?
The handbook says students seeking a peer tutor should contact the department chair or the director of studies. [source: handbook]
Can Webb faculty be paid to tutor Webb students without approval?
No. The handbook says Webb faculty and staff may not be compensated for tutoring Webb students without prior approval from the director of studies. [source: handbook]
Where can on-campus tutoring take place?
The handbook says tutoring takes place in Fawcett Library classrooms, the Stockdale Community Center, or designated music practice space. [source: handbook]
What is one reason Webb gives for discouraging too much online tutoring?
The handbook says overreliance on online tutoring can lead to miscommunication with teachers and may lead to academic integrity violations. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about repeated missing work after an absence?

The handbook says students are generally expected to have as many class rotations to complete missed work as they were absent, unless an extended health leave contract says otherwise. [source: handbook]
How does Webb describe afternoon participation overall?

The course catalog says all students participate in the afternoon program each season of the year, and every student must complete at least one season of interscholastic sport each year. [source: course catalog]
What kind of support does Webb say its advisory program provides?
The admissions page says the advisory program supports students socially, emotionally, and academically. [source: admissions]
